explanation: The property states that globally, whenever V_Mot is 0 (bool_exp1), then Spd_Act is 0 (bool_exp3) must also hold. This directly captures the 'While A, then B' implication.

The property states that globally, whenever 'V_Mot is 0' is true, it implies that 'Spd_Act is 0' must also be true at the same moment. This captures the 'While A, then B' meaning as a global implication.

The property states that globally, whenever the motor voltage (V_Mot) is 0, the actual speed (Spd_Act) must also be 0. This directly captures the "While A, then B" relationship using a global implication.

The property states that globally, whenever 'V_Mot is 0' (bool_exp1) holds, 'Spd_Act is 0' (bool_exp3) must also hold at the same time. This directly translates the 'While A, then B' structure as 'Globally (A implies B)'.

The phrase "While V_Mot is 0, then..." indicates a global property, meaning that at all times (G), if V_Mot is 0 (bool_exp1), then Spd_Act is 0 (bool_exp3) must hold. This is a direct global implication.

The property states that globally, whenever 'V_Mot is 0' (bool_exp1) is true, then 'Spd_Act is 0' (bool_exp3) must also be true. This directly captures the 'While A, B holds' meaning, ensuring that the second condition is always met as long as the first condition is active.

The property states that globally, at any point in time, if V_Mot is 0 (bool_exp1) then Spd_Act is 0 (bool_exp3) at that same point in time. This directly translates the 'while A, then B' condition as a global implication.

The property states that globally (G), if 'V_Mot is 0' (bool_exp2) is true, then it implies that 'Spd_Act is 0' (bool_exp3) must also be true. This directly captures the "While P, then Q" meaning, indicating an invariant implication.

The property 'While A, then B' is translated into a global implication 'G (A -> B)'. In this case, 'A' is 'V_Mot is 0' and 'B' is 'Spd_Act is 0'. Thus, the LTL formula states that globally, if V_Mot is equal to 0, then Spd_Act must also be equal to 0.

The natural language phrase "While V_Mot is 0, then all of the following conditions hold: Spd_Act is 0." translates to a global implication. This means that at all times, if 'V_Mot is 0' (bool_exp1) is true, then 'Spd_Act is 0' (bool_exp3) must also be true. Template 30, which is G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), perfectly captures this relationship.
